meta_title: For Founders: portfolio building to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Portfolio Building to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop watching tutorials and start shipping. Master portfolio building with Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your SaaS MVP faster than your competitors.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop consuming and start shipping. By focusing on portfolio building—creating functional, revenue-ready micro-SaaS projects—you convert passive knowledge into tangible assets. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code to iterate your production logic, ensuring every line of code serves a validated business requirement.
content.intro:
The screen glows with another 'To-Do List' tutorial, but your bank account remains stagnant. You are trapped in the high-definition purgatory of Tutorial Hell, where you feel productive while your actual product remains a ghost in your codebase. Every hour spent following a guided video is an hour stolen from your market validation.

It is time to pivot from passive observer to aggressive architect. Portfolio building is the only framework that bridges the gap between 'knowing how to code' and 'building a profitable SaaS.' By leveraging AI-native tooling, you will strip away the boilerplate and focus purely on the business logic that users will actually pay for.

steps.title: Define Your MVP Value Proposition
steps.description: Before touching code, identify a single, painful problem you can solve in 72 hours. Focus on one core feature that provides immediate utility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Review my problem statement: [INSERT PROBLEM]. Generate a lean database schema and a core feature set for a 3-day MVP build.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Analyze this schema and suggest the most efficient tech stack for rapid deployment.'

steps.title: Scaffold with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate code, authentication flow, and payment integration. Don't write what you can generate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a full-stack scaffold using Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase. Include user authentication and a Stripe billing integration skeleton.

steps.title: Implement Core Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to handle the complex business logic, ensuring your code adheres to production-grade security and scalability standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure API route for handling user data processing. Ensure error handling is robust and follows RESTful best practices.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --file src/app/api/process/route.ts --logic 'Implement secure data handling with Zod validation'

steps.title: Deploy to Production Instantly
steps.description: Stop testing on localhost. Deploy immediately to a cloud provider to start gathering real-world feedback.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a step-by-step CI/CD pipeline configuration for Vercel deployment with automated environment variable injection.

steps.title: Iterate Based on User Metrics
steps.description: Once live, use your AI tools to analyze user feedback and iterate the product. This is where the real SaaS building begins.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze these user feedback logs: [PASTE LOGS]. Suggest the top three features to prioritize for the next iteration to increase conversion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor --file src/components/Dashboard.tsx --instruction 'Implement user-requested feedback changes'

pro_tips: Never build authentication from scratch; use an identity provider to reclaim 20 hours of development time.
Focus on 'Time-to-First-Dollar' over feature parity; if it doesn't solve a payment-worthy problem, delete it.
Use AI to write your unit tests; it prevents regressions during rapid iteration cycles.
Treat your codebase like a startup: keep it lean, remove dead code weekly, and avoid premature scaling.
Build in public; sharing your progress on social media creates an audience that becomes your first beta testers.
content.next_steps: After deploying your MVP, set up a simple analytics dashboard to track user behavior. Reach out to your first 10 potential users and offer them lifetime access in exchange for rigorous feedback.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the framework to move beyond theory and build a business. Stop waiting for permission to be successful. The market is waiting for your solution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
